concept.name: implicit rows
concept.explain:
Grid can create new rows automatically when you place an item outside the current grid.

Example:
grid-row: 8 / 9;

This places the car on a new implicit row.

ui.prompt: Goal: Move the car to row 8 while keeping it in column 2.
hints: Use grid-row to place the car outside the current grid.

entities.baseCss: background: rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.22); outline: 2px dashed rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.8); border-radius: 10px;

concept.name: implicit columns
concept.explain:
Grid can create new columns automatically when you place an item outside the current grid.

Example:
grid-column: 8 / 9;

This places the car on a new implicit column.

ui.prompt: Goal: Move the car to column 8 while keeping it on row 4.
hints: Use grid-column to place the car outside the current grid.

entities.baseCss: background: rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.22); outline: 2px dashed rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.8); border-radius: 10px;

concept.name: grid-auto-rows and grid-auto-columns
concept.explain:
Use grid-auto-rows and grid-auto-columns to control the size of implicit tracks.

Example:
grid-auto-rows: 80px;

This gives new implicit rows a fixed size instead of using the default.

ui.prompt: Goal: Keep the car on row 8 and make the new implicit row match the highlighted parking bay.
hints: Use grid-auto-rows to size the new implicit row.
Only the new row needs to become taller.

entities.baseCss: background: rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.22); outline: 2px dashed rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.8); border-radius: 10px; min-height: 80px; align-self: start;

concept.name: explicit vs implicit tracks
concept.explain:
Implicit tracks appear automatically when needed. Explicit tracks are the ones you define yourself with grid-template-columns and grid-template-rows.

You can turn an implicit layout into an explicit one by defining the full grid.

ui.prompt: Goal: Park in row 8 / column 8, then define an explicit 8×8 grid.
hints: First place the car at column 8 and row 8.
Then define 8 equal columns and 8 equal rows.

entities.baseCss: background: rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.22); outline: 2px dashed rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.8); border-radius: 10px;
